title: Two-Player and Bot-vs-Bot Multiplayer Modes
content:
## Overview

The Snake game supports three game modes, gated by a `GameMode` type (`single`, `pvp`, `bvb`) introduced in `src/main.ts`. Two-player (PvP) mode lets two people play on the same keyboard simultaneously; bot-vs-bot (BvB) mode is a hands-off spectator experience where two independently configured bots compete.

---

## Two-Player (PvP) Mode

### Entry Point
A **"Two Player"** button on the main menu launches PvP mode. The game loop starts immediately without waiting for a keypress (same behavior as BvB).

### Snake Setup
Both snakes are placed on opposite sides of the grid at game start. Player 2 has its own full state: snake positions, direction, score, and an occupied-cell set, mirroring Player 1's state.

### Controls
- **Player 1**: WASD only
- **Player 2**: Arrow keys only

In single-player mode both key sets remain available. In two-player mode each player is locked to their own set to prevent one player's input from accidentally moving the other's snake.

### Game Loop
`updateTwoPlayer()` runs as a parallel game-loop path alongside the existing `updateSinglePlayer()`. Each tick it:
1. Computes both snakes' next head positions before applying either move.
2. Checks wall collisions, self-collisions, and cross-snake collisions simultaneously.
3. Treats a head-to-head collision as a **draw** rather than giving priority to whichever snake was evaluated first.

### Shared Food and Grid
Both snakes compete for the same single food item on a shared grid. The existing food-spawning and grid-expansion logic is reused with minor adjustments to account for combined snake length.

### Game Over
`endGameTwoPlayer()` determines the winner and populates the game-over screen with:
- A winner announcement (or draw declaration).
- Per-player final scores.
- The all-time high score is hidden (irrelevant in head-to-head play).

---

## Bot-vs-Bot (BvB) Mode

### Entry Point
A **"Bot vs Bot"** button on the main menu, alongside a second bot selector, lets the player independently choose a bot profile for each snake before launching. The game loop starts immediately and the isometric auto-rotation activates automatically (matching existing single-player demo behavior).

### Bot Architecture
Each bot runs its own decision function via `chooseBotDirectionForPlayer()`, added to `src/main.ts`. This method:
- Builds a `BotState` that includes the opponent's snake positions.
- Passes a custom `BotHelpers` object whose `simulateMove` and `analyzePosition` calls treat the opponent's body as additional blocked cells.

The `analyzePosition()` helper was updated to accept an optional extra set of blocked positions, preserving existing single-player behavior when the argument is omitted.

### Type Changes (`src/bots/bot-types.ts`)
`BotState` was extended with an optional `opponentSnake` field. Making it optional preserves full backward compatibility with existing single-player bot implementations.

Player 2 has its own dedicated bot instance (`activeBot2`, `selectedBotId2`) in `src/main.ts`.

---

## UI Changes

### Menu (`index.html`, `src/style.css`)
- "Two Player" and "Bot vs Bot" buttons added alongside "New Game".
- A second bot selector panel added so each snake's bot profile can be configured independently before a BvB match.
- Bot controls (toggle button and profile selector) are **hidden entirely** in two-player modes rather than disabled, to avoid confusion about whether a bot is influencing play.

### In-Game HUD
`updateModeUI()` in `src/main.ts` toggles UI elements by mode:
- **Single-player**: existing score display and bot toggle.
- **Two-player modes**: per-player scores (Player 1 in green, Player 2 in blue) and bot controls hidden.
- Instructions text updates to reflect the active control scheme.

### Game-Over Screen
- Shows winner announcement and per-player scores (color-coded) in two-player modes.
- Hides all-time high score in two-player modes.
- A **"Menu"** button and an **Escape** key shortcut return to the main menu from any screen.

---

## Files Modified
| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `src/main.ts` | `GameMode` type; Player 2 state; `updateTwoPlayer()`; `endGameTwoPlayer()`; `chooseBotDirectionForPlayer()`; `updateModeUI()`; `startNewGame()` extended for multi-snake modes |
| `src/bots/bot-types.ts` | Optional `opponentSnake` field on `BotState` |
| `index.html` | New mode buttons; second bot selector; per-player score elements; winner announcement element; Menu button |
| `src/style.css` | Per-player score styling (green/blue); visibility rules for mode-specific elements |
